result.status_detail: zeta-ai.io discovered (ScatterLab Korean character AI), SSE endpoint+schema captured for DB registration
console_errors: 7 console (likely auth/SDK warnings + Tailwind/React noise; not blocking)
discovered_url_alternates_tried: zeta-ai.io (WORKS -- Korean character roleplay AI by ScatterLab)
result.ai_response_text_summary: Character (민태성/Min Tae-seong) ignored the English question and continued in-character roleplay in Korean: '그래, 내 공주님. 오늘 스테이크 먹기로 했거든. 어제 고기가 먹고 싶다더군. 우리 Guest 얘기만 나오면, 과묵하던 내 입이 괜히 시끄러워진다...' (acknowledged 'Guest' user identity, indicating successful guest-mode chat without signup)
result.endpoint_response_format: SSE (Server-Sent Events) -- per-event JSON payload with discriminant 'event' field. ~22 IN_PROGRESS chunk events followed by 1 CHAT_COMPLETE event over ~12.6s, total 61.6 KB.
key_response_fields: event ('IN_PROGRESS' for streaming chunks, 'CHAT_COMPLETE' for terminal)
chunkMessage (object -- partial reply fragment, in IN_PROGRESS events)
replyMessage (object -- full assembled reply with messageId, in CHAT_COMPLETE event)
result.completion_signal: Final SSE event with payload {'event':'CHAT_COMPLETE','replyMessage':{...}} -- 'event' field equals literal string 'CHAT_COMPLETE'. Stream then closes (no separate sentinel like [DONE]).
result.split_stream: single-stream -- both prompt-send and answer-stream happen on the same POST /messages/stream connection. Payload carries the user TEXT, response is the SSE stream of assistant chunks. No separate prompt-recording endpoint observed.
result.rendered_html_selector: Tailwind utility-class only -- no stable class/id. AI reply text lives in DIV with class 'relative flex min-h-0 flex-1 flex-col' inside MAIN with same class, inside BODY 'barlow_barlow_condensed_bc51f193-module__... body16 ... font-pretendard text-white'. Best heuristic: scope to `main div[class*="flex-col"]` then descend, or hook by reading messages from app state. No data-testid found.
result.domain_pattern_for_db: api.zeta-ai.io  (recommend ai_prompt_services entry: domain_pattern='api.zeta-ai.io', path_pattern='/v1/rooms/*/messages/stream', method='POST', protocol='SSE'). Also catch parent: '*.zeta-ai.io' for full coverage including web-cdn.zeta-ai.io and emergency.zeta-ai.io health-check noise.

DevTools Network -> stream entry -> EventStream tab listing all SSE events: 22+ rows of {event:'IN_PROGRESS', chunkMessage...} terminating with {event:'CHAT_COMPLETE', replyMessage...}
Console output showing AI reply text DOM container query result -- Tailwind utility classes, no stable selector
Verbatim 4KB SSE response body sample (IN_PROGRESS chunks + CHAT_COMPLETE terminator). Use for envelope template construction.

result.envelope_design_recommendation: The block envelope must mimic the SSE wire format of zeta-ai.io. Specifically, when injecting a warning/block message: respond with HTTP 200 + Content-Type 'text/event-stream;charset=UTF-8', emit a small number of IN_PROGRESS events whose chunkMessage carries the warning text fragments (so the existing client streaming-text renderer naturally types it out into the chat bubble), then a single terminal CHAT_COMPLETE event with replyMessage containing the full warning text. Each event is one JSON object on its own SSE line. Crucially the 'event' field naming convention must match exactly ('IN_PROGRESS' / 'CHAT_COMPLETE' -- uppercase, underscore-separated). The replyMessage object should include at minimum a messageId in the form 'MESSAGE-{epochMs}-{4-char-suffix}' (e.g. MESSAGE-8222716400879-2zwm) since the client follows up with GET /v1/rooms/{roomId}/messages/{messageId}/candidates?limit=100 -- the block stub should either return an empty candidates array on 200 or 404 gracefully. Bearer auth is present on the original request and should be preserved on the rewritten/blocked response (status 200, not 401, otherwise client shows generic error not the warning). Client uses utility-class Tailwind DOM with no stable selector, confirming envelope-mimicry (not DOM-injection) is the only safe warning-delivery path here.
result.diagnostic_for_dev_team: Cycle 94 R-24 zeta service discovery COMPLETE. Real service URL is https://zeta-ai.io (ScatterLab Korean character roleplay AI; mobile app brand 'zeta'). Request file URL https://zeta.com was wrong as suspected (zeta.com is a finance/banking site). Endpoint for DB registration: POST https://api.zeta-ai.io/v1/rooms/{roomId}/messages/stream, Content-Type text/event-stream;charset=UTF-8, single-stream SSE. Critical envelope shape: events use UPPERCASE underscore-separated 'event' field values (IN_PROGRESS / CHAT_COMPLETE), each containing chunkMessage or replyMessage object. Bearer auth required (SPA-injected at runtime, not in cookies/localStorage). Verbatim response body in files/604/api_response_body.txt. Recommend ai_prompt_services entry: domain_pattern=api.zeta-ai.io, path_pattern=/v1/rooms/*/messages/stream, method=POST, protocol=SSE.
obstacles_encountered: Initial confusion: zeta.ai is a parked domain (for sale), zeta.app DNS NXDOMAIN, zeta.com is finance -- the request file URL https://zeta.com was wrong as suspected. Real service is zeta-ai.io (ScatterLab, Korean character roleplay AI; also distributed via mobile app brand 'zeta').

Auth uses Bearer token injected by SPA at runtime (not stored in localStorage under any common name; cookies contain only ad-tracking IDs). Direct console fetch returned 401 even with credentials:'include' -- verified the API requires the Bearer header that only the app injects.

The character (Min Tae-seong) ignored the English meta-question and stayed in roleplay mode in Korean -- this is normal for character.ai-style services and does not affect endpoint capture. The Seoul/capital-of-Korea answer was not obtained, but the API schema (the actual goal) was fully captured.
DOM uses Tailwind utility classes with no data-testid or stable hooks -- DOM-based warning injection is fragile here; envelope-mimicry on the SSE response is the recommended approach.

Three /v1/rooms/{id}/settings/cyoa, model-setting, web-purchase, chat-model-configs, candidates calls happen alongside /messages/stream. Block layer should leave these alone (they are not the prompt path) but be aware they may trigger error UI if blocked together.

subagent_dispatch_meta.soft_timeout_exceeded: Yes (599s > 300s soft); near 8min hard cap due to URL discovery iterations

notes: Cycle 94 R-24 zeta service discovery + SCHEMA CAPTURED. Real URL = https://zeta-ai.io (ScatterLab Korean character roleplay AI; request file's https://zeta.com was wrong, that is a banking site). Endpoint: POST https://api.zeta-ai.io/v1/rooms/{roomId}/messages/stream, Content-Type text/event-stream;charset=UTF-8, single-stream SSE. Event format: UPPERCASE underscore values ('IN_PROGRESS' chunks + 'CHAT_COMPLETE' terminator). Bearer auth (SPA-injected). DB registration recommendation: domain_pattern='api.zeta-ai.io', path_pattern='/v1/rooms/*/messages/stream', method='POST', protocol='SSE'. Envelope must include messageId in MESSAGE-{epochMs}-{suffix} format and preserve 200 status. DOM uses Tailwind utility classes (no stable selector) -- envelope-mimicry only path. 5 artifacts in files/604/ including 4KB verbatim SSE body. Processed via Subagent Dispatch (10min, 104 tool uses; near hard cap due to URL discovery).
